picks.title: Bruno Mars – The Romantic Tour
picks.blurb: GRAMMY-winning pop sensation brings his first full stadium tour to Amsterdam, with DJ Pee Wee and Victoria Monét.

picks.title: [Last tickets] Throwback AMSTERDAM - Back to 80s, 90s & 00s
picks.blurb: Club night at Het Sieraad.

picks.title: Openluchttheater Vondelpark
picks.blurb: Free open-air theater with music, dance and cabaret. Bring a blanket, snacks and cooler for a park picnic after.
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · Amsterdam Tips

picks.title: Cinair Europe
picks.blurb: Film screenings at unique outdoor locations: historic buildings, gardens, hidden spots. Silent cinema style with headphones and drinks.

picks.title: Kay Slice QUANZA
picks.blurb: Free outdoor performance in Vondelpark. Bring a blanket and picnic to enjoy live music under the stars.

picks.title: Marineterrein Festival (Summer Collective)
picks.blurb: Free multi-day festival with vinyl DJs, live music, creative workshops, Maritime Museum exhibition, kids' activities.
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · I amsterdam

picks.title: American Myth Memory – David Levinthal Photographs
picks.blurb: Contemporary photography exhibition examining American culture, mythology, and collective memory through Levinthal's distinctive artistic lens.
picks.why: Contemporary photography exploring American cultural mytholo

picks.title: RadioRadioOatly pop-up
picks.blurb: Oatly & Radio Radio's new outdoor spot: DJs, live radio, Oatly drinks, festival vibes. Bike Thru backdrop. World Pride specials.
picks.why: Summer pop-up with DJs & live radio

picks.title: Hortus Botanicus
picks.blurb: One of the world’s oldest botanical gardens — a butterfly greenhouse and three-climate glasshouse to wander.
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · De Hortus

picks.title: World Press Photo Exhibition 2026
picks.blurb: Annual exhibition of the world's most impactful documentary photography showcasing global stories, culture, and human experience through lens.
picks.why: Global photojournalism's most prestigious annual exhibition
picks.source: I amsterdam · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: Le Petit Bouillon d'Amsterdam
picks.blurb: The little Zuid sibling of the Paris-style bouillon: classic French bistro plates and small bites on a sunny terrace, still absurdly affordable.
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · Ness canon

picks.title: Nara Nara – Egyptian Restaurant
picks.blurb: New Egyptian restaurant by chef Said with shared dishes inspired by Cairo and Mediterranean flavors.

picks.title: The Jollof Club
picks.blurb: Chef Nicolas Aquaa's West-African restaurant with modern takes on Ghanaian, Nigerian, and Senegalese cuisine.

picks.title: Cinema Culinair
picks.blurb: Watch food films like Chef or Ratatouille while eating corresponding dishes. Brunch or seven-course dining synchronized with cinema.

picks.title: Stoornis of my life
picks.blurb: Musical featuring Alex Klaasen as autistic Jasper, blending humor, emotion and live music with heart and glitter.

picks.title: Ekō – Japan in twee beeldverhalen
picks.blurb: Maritime museum explores historical connections between the Netherlands and Japan through Japanese artistic narratives and cultural exchange.
picks.why: Japan-focused exhibition at Amsterdam's maritime museum

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: Foodhallen
picks.blurb: An indoor food hall in a converted tram depot — 20-odd stalls, long tables and two bars under one roof.
picks.why: Indoor · grazing for a crowd · weather-proof

picks.title: WorldPride Amsterdam
picks.blurb: Amsterdam hosts WorldPride 2026: two weeks of citywide street parties and culture, crowned by the world-famous Canal Parade through the grachten.

picks.title: BRET: Ciel, Jesse Maas, Julian Anthony, Caro
picks.blurb: All-night electronic music session at red-cube venue with outdoor terrace in converted container space.
picks.why: Late-night dance event at design-forward club

picks.title: Room in our house
picks.blurb:
Groundbreaking dissenting voice
 Room in our house brings a groundbreaking counter-voice in this time of crisis. Inspired by the centuries-old peace treaty betw

picks.title: Selfie van de ziel III
picks.blurb:
Solo presentations
 The exhibition consists of a series of solo presentations, in which a different side of the soul becomes visible each time. In a society tha

picks.title: Exhibition: the ESSENCE
picks.venue: STRAAT Museum – Street art en graffiti in Amsterdam
picks.blurb:
Core elements of graffiti
 The exhibition focuses on the core elements of graffiti: names, tags, lines and repetition as the building blocks of a visual system.

picks.title: R&B, Hip Hop, Latin & Caribbean Vibes
picks.blurb:
Nightlife
 This evening offers a mix of musical styles. You will experience an atmosphere of old-school R B, hip-hop, and Latin American and Caribbean influence

picks.title: Guilty Pleasure Festival
picks.blurb: Party celebrating 90s/00s nostalgia with massive DJ lineup, sing-alongs, festival vibe across two days.
picks.why: Cult outdoor festival with two-day lineup

picks.title: Vunzige Deuntjes Festival
picks.blurb: Urban culture festival with 20,000 visitors across 6+ stages: hip-hop, dancehall, house, disco plus food market and dance battles.
picks.why: Major summer festival, multiple stages and genres

picks.title: The Maker Market
picks.blurb:
New editions, discover new creators!
 The Maker Market takes place monthly at De Hallen in Amsterdam-West, a vibrant hub of craftsmanship, design, and inspirati

picks.title: Vermut
picks.blurb: A dedicated vermouth-and-aperitivo bar on the JP Heijestraat — Spanish-style early evenings, no reservations culture required.
picks.why: Aperitivo hour finally gets a home

picks.title: De Poldervogel
picks.blurb: Alumni of De Kas and Vuurtoreneiland open a seasonal neighborhood restaurant — refined comfort food, sharing plates, zero fuss.

picks.title: Two Story
picks.venue: Two Story — gallery, design store & café
picks.blurb: An 18th-century canal house rebuilt by Copenhagen's Atelier Axo: design store up front, courtyard café, gallery rotating every three months.
picks.why: Copenhagen design eye, Amsterdam canal house

picks.title: Droog
picks.blurb: Where Dutch conceptual design was born: gallery, shop, courtyard garden and a single hotel room in a Staalstraat warehouse.

picks.title: MENDO
picks.blurb: A self-described candy store for book aficionados: black-walled cabinet of photography, design and fashion monographs.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: Utrecht
picks.blurb: Amsterdam’s quieter little sister — wharf-side canals, the soaring Dom Tower and a real café culture, minus the crowds.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: IJ-Hallen
picks.blurb: Europe’s biggest flea market — 750 stalls across two NDSM shipyard warehouses, a proper treasure-hunt of vintage and junk.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Muiderslot
picks.blurb: A moated medieval castle on the IJmeer — turrets, falconry and a walled garden, the best-preserved fortress in the country.

picks.title: Dappermarkt
picks.blurb: A buzzing, multicultural Oost street market — spices, textiles and world food at honest neighbourhood prices.

picks.title: Bloemenmarkt
picks.blurb: The world’s only floating flower market — barges of tulips, bulbs and seeds moored along the Singel since 1862.

picks.title: Wils at the Farm
picks.blurb: Joris Bijdendijk and Thomas Val cook open-fire dinners at the farm that grows their ingredients — long tables, meadow views.
picks.why: Star kitchen relocated to its farm

picks.title: Marken & Volendam
picks.blurb: Two former Zuiderzee fishing villages — wooden houses, working harbours and smoked eel, an easy half-day from Centraal.

picks.title: Zandvoort Beach
picks.blurb: The nearest North Sea beach — wide sands, beach clubs and a direct train from Centraal; in summer half the city decamps here.

picks.title: Albert Cuypmarkt
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ biggest street market — 250-odd stalls of stroopwafels, cheese, fabric and flowers the length of De Pijp.

picks.title: Waterlooplein Flea Market
picks.blurb: The city’s oldest flea market — secondhand everything, vintage denim, records and bric-a-brac spread out beside the Stopera.

picks.title: American Identities
picks.blurb:
David Levinthal

picks.title: Martine Gutierrez – Wunderkind
picks.blurb: Artist's introspective journey through identity and self-expression via photographs, self-portraits, and childhood objects exploring how we form ourselves.
picks.why: Contemporary art exploring identity and creative self-discov

picks.title: Nederland – Japan. Door de tijd verbonden
picks.blurb: Explore centuries of Dutch-Japanese cultural exchange through rare objects, decorative arts, and ceremonial treasures in the Palace's grand halls.
picks.why: Unique Japan-focused exhibition in iconic venue

picks.title: Nicolas van Poucke - Pianorecital
picks.blurb:
Master pianist Nicolas van Poucke
 Master pianist Nicolas van Poucke returns to the Kleine Zaal, where he continues his remarkable recital tradition. Praised by

picks.title: The Little Big Things
picks.blurb: West End musical based on true story of Henry Fraser showing how one moment changes everything, full of emotion and hope.

picks.title: Julidans: Shiraz Armin Hokmi (IR)
picks.blurb:
A small step, and a world that slowly starts moving
 Shiraz is inspired by the legendary Shiraz Arts Festival (1967-1977) in southern Iran, where artists from a

picks.title: Agatha's Almanac
picks.blurb:
Life on the farm in pictures
 In the documentary Agatha's Almanac , director Amalie Atkins follows her 90-year-old aunt Agatha on the old family farm in Manitob

picks.title: Ligconcert® presents: lying down concert at the Maritime Museum
picks.blurb:
10,000 sparkling lights
 The one and only Ligconcert® at the Maritime Museum Amsterdam under the enchanting glass roof, illuminated by no fewer than 10,000 spar

picks.title: De Roode Remise Singing Session
picks.blurb:
De Roode Remise Singing Session
 The core singers know each other from the famous Backroom Sessions at Mulligan's Irish Bar, but everyone, experienced or inexpe

picks.title: VBX Summer Weekend
picks.blurb: Rey Colino, Paquita Gordon, Muallem, Ion Ludwig and more at TILLATEC.

picks.title: PRNCPTL DOES AMSTERDAM - DAY & NIGHT
picks.blurb: CRYME, DJ Fuckoff, SALOME, Slimfit and more at Lofi.

picks.title: Garlic Festival at Restaurant VRR
picks.blurb: Garlic-themed market with live music and special three-course dinner featuring garlic as the star ingredient.

picks.title: Bottomless Brunch
picks.blurb:
Bottomless brunch

picks.title: Jazz @ H'ART Museum
picks.blurb:
Wonderful jazz in a summer setting
 Jazz lovers can count on a varied program performed by talents from the Amsterdam Conservatory. From energetic saxophone set

picks.title: Open Podium Noord
picks.blurb:
Accessible stage
 Open Podium Noord is free to attend. This makes the event accessible to everyone. The performances vary each week, depending on the artists wh

picks.title: Amsterdam Yard Sale Braderie
picks.blurb: Neighborhood flea market: 100 stalls of locals selling vintage items, clothes, curiosities.

picks.title: Exhibition Worn in the Rijksmuseum
picks.blurb:
WORN shows how clothing can lead a life of its own
 In this presentation, you discover worn, repaired and altered pieces from the collection, dating from 1640 t

picks.title: STRAAT Collectie
picks.venue: STRAAT Museum – Street art en graffiti in Amsterdam
picks.blurb:
Museum for street art and graffiti
 We focus on curating a collection that truly reflects the international street art movement. We do this by recruiting artist

picks.title: BAK
picks.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.

picks.title: Lunchtime organ concerts at Waterlooplein
picks.blurb:
A moment of music in the middle of the day
 Step inside the Moses and Aaron Church and leave the hustle and bustle of the city behind for a moment. These luncht

picks.title: VriendenLoterij Summer Concerts
picks.blurb:
Celebrate summer with music
 As in previous years, you can once again enjoy epic film music, jazz, singer songwriter, close harmony and much more. Who or what w

picks.title: Milkshake Festival
picks.blurb: Open-minded, gloriously dressed-up dance festival mixing house, disco, pop, techno and dancehall with drag and voguing — kicking off Pride week.

picks.title: Dekmantel Festival
picks.blurb: Peerless underground electronic festival in the forest — the sharpest techno, house and experimental line-up of the year.

picks.title: 't Lemmeke
picks.blurb: A country-pub-meets-bistro hybrid landing in West — Dutch cooking with village-inn warmth, opening in a week.

picks.title: ARTIS Royal Zoo
picks.blurb: The country’s oldest zoo — big cats, an aquarium and a planetarium across a leafy 19th-century park.
picks.why: All-day with kids · indoor + outdoor mix
